verified_by: Editor cross-referenced against WHO DON599, France 24, NPR, Al Jazeera
outbreak_summary: Active 2026 Andes hantavirus cluster linked to the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius. Index patients had travelled in Argentina before boarding on 1 April 2026. Three deaths reported, one patient in ICU, additional confirmed case in Switzerland.
case_breakdown: 3 confirmed (2 originally on ship + 1 in Switzerland), 5 suspected (3 still aboard, 2 evacuated). Of the 3 deaths: Case 1 died aboard, Case 2 died in Johannesburg, Case 4 died aboard on 2 May 2026.
sources: WHO Disease Outbreak News DON599 (4 May 2026)

locations.name: MV Hondius (off Cabo Verde)
locations.description: Cluster epicentre. Dutch-flagged vessel with 147 people aboard (88 passengers, 59 crew, 23 nationalities). 2 deaths occurred aboard.

locations.name: Johannesburg, South Africa
locations.description: Evacuation destination. Case 2 died here. Case 3 in ICU here.

locations.name: Zurich, Switzerland
locations.description: 1 confirmed case. Swiss couple disembarked at Saint Helena and returned home end of April; partner being treated.

locations.name: Argentina (suspected exposure origin)
locations.description: Cases 1 and 2 had travelled in Argentina prior to boarding. Andes strain is endemic to Argentina and Chile.
